steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence says “One way to ______ the importance of a scholar's research is to track how often other scholars cite it.” Tracking citations is a measuring or evaluating action, so the blank needs a word that means to judge or evaluate something's significance.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — utilize: Utilize means to make use of something. You can't “utilize the importance” — that doesn't describe what citation-tracking does. ✗
  • Choice B — influence: Influence means to affect something. Tracking citations doesn't influence importance — it reveals it. ✗
  • Choice C — undermine: Undermine means to weaken. The passage presents citation-tracking as revealing significance, not destroying it. ✗
  • Choice D — assess: Assess means to evaluate. Tracking how often a scholar is cited is precisely how you assess (judge the value of) their work's importance. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
